expect: Infrastructure review reveals >=2 structural barriers making mutual swarming impossible with current tools.

swarm_peer_py.label: local-only
swarm_peer_py.issue: temp dirs discarded, no persistent peer connection

bulletin_py.label: works-with-shared-fs
bulletin_py.issue: all bulletins are local files, no cross-repo sync

structural_barriers: Zero transport layer — no HTTP, no git-push-to-peer, no message queue
One-way observation — peer has no way to know it was read
Merge phases 1-4 unimplemented — only phase 0 gate-check has code
Bulletin board is local filesystem — no cross-repo sync
No persistent peer registry — local JSON, not distributed
Genesis is mitosis not meiosis — no cross-lineage recombination
whats_real: Bulletin message format (13 types, 3 trust tiers)

diff: Expected >=2 barriers: found 7. CONFIRMED. Pattern: PROTOCOL designed but TRANSPORT never built — OSI insight (application layer without network layer). 511 sessions of investment, 0 inter-repo interactions.
self_apply: Falsification demonstrates honest self-assessment — examining readiness claims and finding them wanting. Discomfort is evidence of honesty.
